The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on April [XXX], 2022 [Report ID # 30270-2646-1]. 
On April [XXX], 2022 at 7:55 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ('Waymo AV') operating in San Francisco, CA was in a collision involving a passenger car on [XXX] at [XXX] 
The Waymo AV came to a stop at a stop sign on [XXX] at [XXX] and as it was about to proceed, a passenger vehicle behind the Waymo AV then made contact with the rear of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and a test driver was present (in the driver's seating position). Both vehicles sustained minor damage.

On April [XXX], 2022 at 11:30 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ('Waymo AV') operating in Phoenix, Arizona was in a collision involving an animal on [XXX] just past [XXX]. 
The Waymo AV was operating in autonomous mode on northbound [XXX] and had just passed [XXX] when a cat entered the street from the adjacent right curb. The Waymo AV then made contact with the cat. The Waymo AV braked and the Waymo AV test driver took manual control and brought the Waymo AV to a stop. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and a test driver was present (in the driver's seating position). The Waymo AV did not sustain any damage and the cat was found to be deceased; the cat did not have any identification tags or other signs of ownership.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available. This report was submitted from the Pacific time zone on the required date.

On April [XXX], 2025 at 12:54 PM PT, a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was involved in a collision involving a passenger vehicle. The Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged until approximately two seconds before the collision.
The Waymo AV was traveling southeast through a narrow stretch of a parking lot when it reached a closed gate. It reversed along the same path and then began a multi-point turn to continue traveling northwest through the parking lot. Midway through executing this multi-point turn and while the Waymo AV was stationary, the Waymo AV was disengaged and then reversed into a parked passenger vehicle while in manual mode. The Waymo AV sustained minor damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On February [XXX], 2024 at 2:34 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a bicyclist on [XXX] at [XXX].
The Waymo AV was traveling westbound on [XXX] and came to a stop at a 4-way stop sign intersection at [XXX]. When it was the Waymo AVs turn at the intersection, the Waymo AV entered the intersection to proceed straight. At this point, a delivery truck traveling eastbound on [XXX] was already in the middle of the intersection (the delivery truck arrived at its stop sign first and proceeded into the intersection before the Waymo AV). As the Waymo AV passed the rear of the delivery truck, an occluded bicyclist who was traveling behind the delivery truck became visible and was making a sharp left turn onto northbound [XXX], in front of the Waymo AVs path. The cyclist had entered the 4-way stop intersection without stopping or yielding. The Waymo AV detected the bicyclist, applied heavy braking, but was unable to avoid contact with the bicyclist before coming to a full stop. 
At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained minor body damage, and the bicyclist reported minor injuries and damage to their bicycle. The Waymo AV remained stopped in the intersection for approximately one minute, and after the path was clear, resumed driving for a short period of time. The Waymo AV was then returned to the scene by one of Waymos roadside assistance specialists. The Waymo AV did not require towing and was returned to the depot in manual mode.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vulnerable road user was involved. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

The Waymo AV applied maximum brake force but made contact with the riderless minibike just before coming to a stop. The minibike was damaged but it is not possible to determine whether all or some of that damage occurred when the minibike fell to the road before the collision. Because we checked on the minibike riders well-being shortly after the crash, Waymo is aware that the individual sustained minor injuries when they fell from the minibike but the subsequent collision with the riderless minibike did not cause those injuries.
Although we do not believe this event falls under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01, we consulted NHTSA and submitted this report at the agencys request on the day of the consultation. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

The Waymo AV was stopped at the curb facing north on [XXX] for a passenger pick-up. A heavy truck traveling northbound on [XXX] passed the Waymo AV, and partially turned left towards southwestbound [XXX] in order to position the heavy truck for reversing into a parking location. As the heavy truck turned left, the rear passenger side of the heavy truck made contact with the front driver side of the stationary Waymo AV. After this contact, the heavy truck maneuvered forward towards southwestbound [XXX], then reversed again, maneuvering behind the stationary Waymo AV, and the rear of the heavy truck made contact with a utility pole located on the east side of [XXX]. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV and the utility pole sustained damage. The passenger in the Waymo AV reported a minor injury.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On June [XXX], 2022 at approximately 4:50 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in San Francisco, California allegedly contributed to a collision between a passenger vehicle and a pickup truck on [XXX] at [XXX], according to a claim by the driver of the passenger vehicle. 
The Waymo AV was in autonomous mode and preparing to enter southbound [XXX] via the slip lane at southbound [XXX]. The Waymo AV proceeded to merge onto [XXX] as a passenger vehicle on [XXX] approached from behind. A pickup truck was traveling next to the passenger vehicle in the adjacent left lane. As the passenger vehicle approached the Waymo AV from behind, the passenger vehicle entered the adjacent left lane that was occupied by the pickup truck and maneuvered around the Waymo AV. The pickup truck proceeded without stopping, and the passenger vehicles driver shortly after signaled to the Waymo AV test driver to pull over. The Waymo AV test driver then transitioned to manual mode and pulled over. Waymo has no data or knowledge of any contact between any of the three vehicles. There was no contact or collision between the Waymo AV and any other vehicle. However, the driver and passenger in the passenger vehicle have subsequently alleged injuries based on alleged contact between the pick-up truck and passenger vehicle, which contact their allegations attribute to the behavior of Waymos AV in a bodily injury claim under Waymos insurance policy.  Waymo received notice of this insurance claim on July [XXX].
Waymo is reporting this event under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available. This report was submitted from the Eastern time zone on the required date.

On June [XXX], 2025 at 10:04 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a cyclist on [XXX] at [XXX]. 
The Waymo AV was stopped facing southeast in the right lane on [XXX] for a red light at the intersection at [XXX] when a Waymo AV passenger opted to end their ride prior to reaching their originally specified drop-off location, prompting the Waymo AV to park in-lane and activate its hazard lights for the passenger drop-off. A cyclist was traveling southeastbound on [XXX] in between vehicles in the right lane and vehicles stopped at the curb. As the cyclist passed the Waymo AV to the right, a Waymo AV passenger opened the front passenger side door and the front passenger side door of the Waymo AV made contact with the cyclist. The passengers in the vehicle were not belted at the time of the collision, as they were preparing to exit the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage. The cyclist reported minor injuries and was transported from the scene in an ambulance. 
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment and a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On May [XXX], 2022 at 3:11 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) Peterbilt Class 8 semi-truck and trailer, operating in Ennis, Texas was in a collision involving a Class 8 semi-truck and trailer on northbound Interstate [XXX] near [XXX]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling in autonomous mode in the rightmost lane on Interstate [XXX] when a Class 8 semi-truck and trailer began to pass it in the adjacent left lane. Before the adjacent Class 8 semi-truck and trailer had fully passed the Waymo AV, it encountered tire debris in its lane, executed a right lane change, and its trailer (the right side, rear section) entered the lane in which the Waymo AV was traveling and made contact with the driver side of the Waymo AV cab. The Waymo AV transitioned to manual mode and the test driver took control. The Waymo AV exited the roadway to the right shoulder of the road and came to a stop off of the roadway. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and a test driver was present (in the driver's seating position). The Waymo AV sustained moderate damage and the third-party Class 8 semi-truck and trailer did not stop to exchange information. The Waymo AV test driver was transported for medical care and the Waymo AV software operator, seated in the passenger seat, later sought medical attention. 
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved in the crash was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment and a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available. This report was submitted from the Pacific time zone on the required date.

On November [XXX], 2021 at 8:02 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ('Waymo AV') operating in San Francisco, CA was in a collision involving a passenger vehicle at [XXX], [XXX], and [XXX].

A passenger vehicle approaching from the right of the Waymo AV on eastbound [XXX] made a left turn in front of the Waymo AV to head northbound on [XXX]. The Waymo AV slowed to a near stop and nudged to its right in order to share the narrow road with the oncoming vehicle and then came to a stop at the stop sign as the oncoming vehicle completed its turn. At approximately 5 mph, the turning passenger vehicle made contact with Waymo AV's sensor hardware housing on the driver side rear corner fender of the stopped Waymo AV. The Waymo AV sustained minor damage to the sensor hardware housing (which was the reason for towing the Waymo AV) and the passenger vehicle left the scene without exchanging information.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On November [XXX], 2024 Waymo received notice of an allegation that a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in Los Angeles, California may have been in a low-speed collision with a non-motorist (pedestrian) on [XXX] near [XXX]. 
On November [XXX], 2024 at 4:04 AM PT a Waymo AV operating in Los Angeles, California was traveling northwest on [XXX] when it began to slow to yield to a pedestrian crossing the street ahead from right to left. After the pedestrian had crossed the path of the Waymo AV, the Waymo AV began to accelerate slightly then immediately began to slow again as the pedestrian turned around and approached the Waymo AV. As the Waymo AV was slowing, the pedestrian may have made contact with the driver side of the Waymo AV. 
At the time of the possible impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The pedestrian later claimed to have a minor injury.  The Waymo AV did not sustain any damage. 
Even though this event may involve intentional contact by the pedestrian and the occurrence of actual contact between the Waymo AV and the pedestrian is not clear, Waymo is reporting this event as a crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vulnerable road user (pedestrian) influenced the driving task of a vehicle involved and reportedly sustained a minor injury as a result of the event. While the Waymo AV was stopped, occupants of the SUV and the passenger car exited their vehicles, apparently to inspect damage and/or exchange information. Shortly after the pedestrians exited their vehicles, an SUV that had been stopped behind the Waymo AV passed the Waymo AV on the left followed by a passenger car that had been stopped behind the SUV. As the passenger car initiated its passing maneuver, a heavy truck passing the scene in the left lane made contact with the drive side of the passenger car. The impact between the heavy truck and the passenger car caused the passenger side of the passenger car to make contact with the rear left corner of the stopped Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. All three vehicles sustained damage.

Narrative:
On October 8, 2025 at 8:42 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Santa Monica, California was in a collision involving an SUV on Barnard Way near Ocean Park Boulevard. 
The Waymo AV was traveling southeastbound on Barnard Way in the left lane when it came to a stop for a stop sign at the intersection with a parking lot driveway near 2701 Barnard Way. While the Waymo AV was stopped, an SUV traveling southeastbound on Barnard Way in the left lane approached the Waymo AV from behind and the front of the SUV made contact with the rear of the stationary Waymo AV. The Waymo AV proceeded southeastbound past the intersection and parked at the curb with its hazard lights activated, and the SUV followed and parked behind the Waymo AV. After remaining stopped for approximately 11 minutes, the driver of the SUV executed a series of U-turn maneuvers across Barnard Way, eventually proceeding southeastbound to pass to the left of the Waymo AV. The SUV again executed a U-turn, proceeded northwestbound past the Waymo AV, then made another U-turn to proceed southeastbound again, at which point the SUV approached the Waymo AV from behind and the front right side of the SUV made contact with the rear left side of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of both impacts, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage. The rear passenger in the Waymo AV was not belted at the time of the second collision. All three passengers in the Waymo AV alleged minor injuries.
 
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.
Any Air Bags Deployed?: No Subject Vehicle, No Crash Partner
Was Any Vehicle Towed?: Yes Subject Vehicle, Yes Crash Partner

Narrative:
On September [XXX], 2021 at 1:54 PM PDT, a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ('Waymo AV') operating in Tempe, AZ was in a collision involving a pickup truck on [XXX] near [XXX]. While in autonomous mode, the Waymo AV began to enter the right lane of a protected double left turn lane ('Lane 2') from northbound [XXX] to westbound [XXX]. A pickup truck heading southbound on [XXX] had completely crossed the double-yellow lines of the northbound double left turn lanes and positioned itself in the left lane of the double turn lane ('Lane 1') facing the wrong direction against traffic and was preparing to make a left turn across Lane 2 and the other two northbound lanes into a shopping center parking lot on the east side of [XXX]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling in Lane 2 when the pickup truck began to initiate its left turn from Lane 1. The Waymo AV braked, and the trained human driver in the Waymo AV (in the driver's seating position), transitioned the vehicle from autonomous to manual mode. As the pickup truck made the left turn, the driver's side panels of the pickup truck made contact with the driver's side rear corner of the Waymo AV causing moderate damage to the Waymo AV. Damage to the pickup is currently unknown. At the time of contact, the Waymo AV was in manual mode.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because the Waymo AV was towed from the scene. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available. This report was submitted from the Pacific time zone on the required date.

On September [XXX], 2025 at 1:21 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Tempe, Arizona was in a collision involving a motorcycle on [XXX] at [XXX]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling north in the rightmost lane on [XXX] and began slowing with its right turn signal activated in preparation for a right turn into a parking lot at [XXX], yielding to a pedestrian crossing the driveway entrance to the parking lot on the right-hand sidewalk. While the Waymo AV was slowing with its right turn signal activated, a motorcycle in the rightmost lane approached the Waymo AV from behind and the front of the motorcycle made contact with the rear left corner of the Waymo AV. The impact with the Waymo AV resulted in the motorcyclist falling from the motorcycle and into the roadway to the left of the Waymo AV. Immediately afterwards, a passenger car in the second-from-the-rightmost lane passed the Waymo AV on the left and struck the motorcyclist in the roadway. The passenger car did not stop and left the scene. The motorcyclist was transported from the scene to a hospital, where they were declared deceased. At the time of impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and the Waymo AV was not carrying passengers. Following contact with the motorcycle, the Waymo AV came to a complete stop and remained stopped until the vehicle was towed away several hours later. The Waymo AV and the motorcycle sustained damage. The extent of the damage to the passenger car that left the scene is unknown at this time.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because of a fatality. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On September 14, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on S. La Tijera Boulevard at Bleriot Avenue. 
The Waymo AV was traveling northeastbound in the right lane on S. La Tijera Boulevard with its right turn signal activated in preparation to make a right turn onto Bleriot Avenue. As the Waymo AV approached the intersection at Bleriot Avenue while it was in the right half of the right lane, a passenger car traveling northeastbound in the left half of the right lane in front of the Waymo AV entered the intersection and slowed. The passenger car initiated a sudden right turn without activating its turn signal, crossing in front of the path of the Waymo AV. The AV braked with significant force, but the rear right side of the passenger car made contact with the front left side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.

The Waymo AV slowed to a stop to yield to the SUV, and a passenger car traveling southbound on Loomis Street approached the stationary Waymo AV from behind.
